title: ayenia rhamnifolia
intro: Ayenia rhamnifolia, also known as "Carrasco's ayenia" or "Wild ayenia," is a flowering plant species in the family Malpighiaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, derived from its roots, leaves, and stems. The health benefits of Ayenia rhamnifolia include treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as skin conditions and digestive problems. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used topically as a poultice or ointment to aid in wound healing and skin infections.
